content.full_text:
California
Fair Political

Practices Commission

April 19, 1990


Honorable Doug Vagim
Supervisor
County of Fresno
P.O. Box 4042
Fresno, CA  93744

                                    Re:  Your Request for Informal Assistance
                                         Our File No. I-90-207

Dear Supervisor Vagim:

     This is in response to your letter requesting advice concern-
ing your responsibilities under the conflict-of-interest provi-
sions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹  Your letter
seeks follow-up advice regarding our advice letter to you of
February 15, 1990.  (Vagim Advice Letter, No. I-89-688, copy
enclosed.)  Since your question is general in nature we are treat-
ing your request as one for informal assistance.²

                              QUESTION

     May you participate in future decisions of the Fresno County
Board of Supervisors concerning the Fresno Metropolitan Flood
Control District?

                              CONCLUSION

     Absent facts to indicate an independent contractual relation-
ship between you and the Fresno Metropolitan Flood Control
District, the district would not be treated as a source of income
to you.  Thus, you may participate in decisions concerning the
Fresno Metropolitan Flood Control District.


_____________________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Govern-
ment Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916)322-5660

File No. I-90-207
Page 2


     Please note, however, that other provisions of law, such as
Section 1090, may restrict your ability to participate in these
decisions. Section 1090 is not within the Political Reform Act,
and is, therefore, outside the Commission's jurisdiction. You
should contact the Attorney General's Office for guidance concern-
ing that section.

                              FACTS

     You are the Supervisor for District 3 of Fresno County. In
your private capacity you provide computer services to the Fresno
Metropolitan Flood Control District (the "district") in conjunc-
tion with your brother's business, Alphameric Data Processing
(hereafter "Alphameric").

     The parties to the contract for provision of computer
services to the district are the district and Alphameric.
However, you were designated in the initial bid as also providing
services to the district on behalf of Alphameric. You described
the payment procedure in connection with the contract as follows:
You submit a listing of charges to Alphameric, Alphameric then
submits a billing to the district which includes your charges, the
district then writes a check to Alphameric, and Alphameric
compensates you for you services from the proceeds of the check.

     You have asked whether you may participate in the following
decisions of the Fresno County Board of Supervisors:

     a.  The distribution of property tax augmentation funds
to the district;

     b.  Proposal to expand the jurisdiction of the district
to other unincorporated regions of the county;

     c.  Transfer of part of the county's property tax base
to the district where the district's jurisdiction is
expanded.

                              ANALYSIS

     Section 87100 of the Act provides:

          No public official at any level of state or
     local government shall make, participate in making
     or in any way attempt to use his official position
     to influence a governmental decision in which he
     knows or has reason to know he has a financial
     interest.

     As a member of the Fresno County Board of Supervisors, you
are a "public official" as defined in the Act. (Section 82048.)
Thus, you may not use your official position to influence a
governmental decision in which you know or have reason to know you
have a financial interest.

File No. I-90-207
Page 3


     Section 87103 specifies that a public official has a
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision will have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on the
official or a member of his or her immediate family or on:

          Any source of income, other than gifts and
     other than loans by a commercial lending institu-
     tion in the regular course of business on terms
     available to the public without regard to official
     status, aggregating two hundred fifty dollars
     ($250) or more in value provided to, received by or
     promised to the public official within 12 months
     prior to the time when the decision is made.

                    Section 87103(c).

     According to Section 87103, any person or business that has
made any payment to you is a source of income to you. In addi-
tion, under some circumstances we have treated multiple persons as
sources of income under a single contract. (Dorsey Advice Letter,
No. A-87-176, copy enclosed.) If, in the aggregate, the payments
are for $250 or more in the past 12 months, the source or sources
are potentially disqualifying financial interests for the purposes
of Section 87103.

     In the previous letter to you we concluded that Alphameric
was the sole source of income to you under the contract between
the district and Alphameric. According to the information you
provided, the district had contracted with Alphameric Data
Processing to provide computer services to the district. You had
no ownership interest in Alphameric, but only act as a consultant
on this contract. Since you appeared to be acting as merely a
subcontractor to Alphameric in providing services to the district
we concluded that the contractor, Alphameric, was the sole source
of income to you, despite the fact that you are paid funds
collected from the district. (See e.g., Hart Advice Letter, No.
A-83-264, copy enclosed.)

     This conclusion is further supported by the fact that the
contract does not appear to create any contractual relationship
between the district and yourself, and only mentions you in the
context of a description of the team to provide services on behalf
of Alphameric. Moreover, your payment flows from Alphameric which
also suggests that the business relationship is actually between
you and Alphameric.

     Thus, absent facts to indicate an independent contractual
relationship between you and the district, we reaffirm our prior
conclusion that the district is not a source of income to you.
Consequently, the Political Reform Act does not prohibit you from
participating in decisions concerning the district. Please note,
however, that other provisions of law, such as Section 1090, may

File No. I-90-207
Page 4

restrict your ability to participate in these decisions. Section
1090 is not within the Commission's jurisdiction. You should contact the
Attorney General's Office for guidance concerning that section.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:JWW:plh

Enclosures
